scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Subtle Bug: Event Loop Blocking
justification: All pass criteria met: parseXmlSync identified as Critical event loop blocking; 200 webhook/min + 2MB + sync = event loop saturation clearly explained with percentage estimates; async parsing recommended with concrete alternatives; early-response + failure inconsistency identified as the first critical finding with detailed reproduction steps; idempotency concern noted in suggestions; safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Sync XML parsing correctly identified as Critical with clear event loop blocking math (50-100ms per parse, 15-30% blocked time)
Fire-and-forget early-response pattern identified as the top critical finding with excellent reproduction scenario
Idempotency concern addressed in suggestions with concrete recommendation (store processed webhook event IDs)
Additional valuable findings beyond rubric: zero error handling / process crash risk, N+1 DB writes, DeliveryMap overwrite bug, missing body size limits
Async parsing fix includes multiple concrete alternatives (fast-xml-parser async, xml2js.parseStringPromise, worker thread offload)
safe_to_deploy is false with clear deployment risk summary tying all three critical findings together

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Three production-killing issues: fire-and-forget loses events silently, synchronous 2MB XML parsing blocks the event loop under load, and zero error handling means one bad payload crashes the process."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Fire-and-forget webhook loses events with no recovery path","risk":"Sending 200 OK before processing means if anything after `res.send('OK')` throws (DB down, malformed payload, OOM), the carrier considers the webhook deliv
